task: The hammer fell onto the workshop floor. It startled the apprentice working quietly at the next bench.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the workshop floor".

task: Caspar showed Bartholomew the old letters. He had inherited them from a distant cousin recently.
The lemon tree grew tall in the garden. It produced fragrant fruit throughout the warmer months every year.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the lemon tree".

task: Genevieve helped Marguerite with the application. She filled out forms while explaining each section carefully.
The wind blew the umbrella down the street. It tumbled past several startled morning pedestrians.
Cassius confronted Brutus about the rumour. He denied any involvement in the scandalous gossip.
The lantern hung above the wooden door. It had welcomed guests to the inn for over two centuries.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the wooden door".

task: Eleanora cooked dinner for Henrietta. She prepared the special family recipe that took several careful hours.
The ribbon decorated the small wrapped box. It had been chosen carefully to match the festive paper.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the small wrapped box".
